images.alt: Line and area chart titled ‘NBA Salary Cap Over Time,’ subtitled ‘Salary cap maximum by league year, 1990–2026.’ The x-axis spans years 1990 to 2026; the y-axis spans $0M to $175M. The line starts near $12M in 1990, rises gradually through the 1990s and 2000s to about $58M by 2009, holds flat through 2013, then climbs sharply after the 2016 cap spike to roughly $94M, continuing upward through the early 2020s to about $112M by 2020, then accelerates again through 2022–2026, ending near $165M in 2026 (matching the NBA’s announced 2026-27 cap of $164.961 million) ￼. The area under the line is shaded light gray. Data source: Spotrac. Viz credit: @HoopInformatics.bsky.social.”
